AEF.notes: French Equatorial Africa represented as a macro-level colonial federation.

ALG.notes: French Algeria represented as a separate colonial administration.

ANG.notes: Portuguese Angola represented as a separate colonial administration.

AOF.notes: French West Africa represented as a macro-level colonial federation.

AOI.notes: Italian East Africa replaces the separate Eritrean and Somali colonial administrations in the 1939 setup.

BEA.notes: British East Africa macro government covering Kenya, Uganda, and Tanganyika.

BRM.notes: Burma converted into a Japanese client state.

BWA.notes: British West Africa macro government covering Nigeria, the Gold Coast, Sierra Leone, and the Gambia.

CEY.notes: Ceylon represented as a distinct British colony.

CHI.notes: 1962 semantic shift for CHI: the Nanjing-centered central Chinese client regime now absorbs the former coastal Reorganized Government core and Hainan.

EGY.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for Egypt in tno_1962.

ENG.notes: British imperial approximation for protectorates, mandates, and colonies outside the dedicated subject governments.

FIN.notes: Aland Islands should follow Finland in 1936.

FRA.notes: French colonial approximation for remaining African and overseas possessions outside the dedicated subject governments.

GER.notes: German 1936 eastern frontier including East Prussia and western territories now inside Poland.

GMA.notes: Gansu-Ningxia Ma sphere after ceding the Alxa banners to Mengjiang.

GRE.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for mainland Greece in tno_1962.

GXC.notes: Guangxi retained as a Japanese-aligned inland client clique.

HOL.notes: Dutch Suriname approximation outside the East Indies.

HUN.notes: Southern Slovak strip from targeted SK ADM2 split features.

INS.notes: Dutch East Indies successor retained as a Japanese client state.

ITA.notes: Italian occupation and annexation of Albania by 1939.

JAP.notes: Jidong Anti-Communist Autonomous Government area is represented as Japanese-controlled territory for 1936 scenario coloring.

JOR.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for Transjordan in tno_1962.

KOR.notes: Formalized Japanese colonial administration over Korea in the 1962 Co-Prosperity Sphere order.

LBA.notes: Italian Libya represented as a distinct colonial administration.

LEB.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for Lebanon in tno_1962.

LIT.notes: Project-specific override: Vilnius remains with Lithuania in this scenario instead of strict vanilla HOI4 ownership.

MAD.notes: Madagascar represented as a separate French colony.

MAL.notes: Malaya and attached dependencies converted into a Japanese client state.

MAN.notes: Manchukuo retained as a formal Japanese protectorate after ceding Dalian to direct Japanese control.

MEN.notes: Expanded Mengjiang covering the TNO-style Chahar-Suiyuan arc, Alxa, and the eastern Mongolian Khentii-Sukhbaatar-Dornod block.

MOR.notes: French Morocco represented as the remaining protectorate area after Spanish Morocco and Spanish Sahara are split out.

MZB.notes: Portuguese Mozambique represented as a separate colonial administration.

PAL.notes: Italian mandate baseline for Palestine in tno_1962.

PHI.notes: Philippines reworked from a United States-associated commonwealth into a Japanese client state.

POL.notes: Eastern Polish territories before 1939 partition, approximated with western Belarus and western Ukraine while leaving the Vilnius project override to Lithuania.

QMA.notes: Qinghai Ma sphere reduced after transferring the Yushu basin and Geermu delegated belt to Tibet.

RAJ.notes: British Raj baseline override for India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh.

RCG.notes: Belgian Congo and Ruanda-Urundi represented as a single Belgian colonial government.

ROM.notes: Greater Romania including Bessarabia and northern Bukovina.

SAF.notes: South West Africa approximation under South African control.

SGN.notes: Spanish Guinea represented as a separate colonial administration.

SHX.notes: Northern inland Shanxi-Shaanxi collaboration regime after ceding the Datong arc to Mengjiang.

SIA.notes: Independent Co-Prosperity ally retained outside the Japanese subject tree while shifting to a darker muted olive.

SLO.notes: Slovakia appears as a separate axis-aligned puppet state in 1939.

SMR.notes: Spanish Morocco represented using the current northern Morocco approximation, with Ifni folded into the same protectorate block.

SOV.notes: USSR baseline override across the modern Soviet successor states before western-border historical corrections.

SPR.notes: Approximate Spanish fallback for Andorra only; colonial holdings are handled by dedicated subject rules.

SSH.notes: Spanish Sahara represented using the current Western Sahara approximation blocks.

SUD.notes: Anglo-Egyptian Sudan represented as a condominium under British primacy with Egyptian linkage retained in metadata.

SYR.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for Syria in tno_1962.

TAI.notes: Formalized Japanese colonial administration over Taiwan in the 1962 Co-Prosperity Sphere order.

TAN.notes: Approximate Tannu Tuva using the current Tuva Republic group.

TUN.notes: Italian protectorate baseline for Tunisia in tno_1962.

TUR.notes: Hatay transfer to Turkey shortly before scenario start.

VIN.notes: Vietnam narrowed to the Vietnamese core after separating Laos and Cambodia.

YUN.notes: Yunnan contracted back to the Yunnan core after separating Guizhou.

RKN.notes: Manual overlay releasable for German Dutch occupation teaching preset.

RKP.notes: Feature-first Generalgouvernement masks. The historical reference footprint is the only exposed RKP boundary, and the German annexation belt is handled as a separate ownership transfer.

RKU.notes: Feature-first Ukraine mask. Historical is the only exposed boundary and includes the northeast, Donbas, Azov corridor, Kirovohrad, and Chernihiv while keeping Transnistria as a separate automatic Romania transfer.

RKO.notes: Feature-first Ostland masks. Warmia-Masuria stays with Germany, the Belarus footprint is resolved explicitly into Ostland, and Marijampole is auto-transferred to Germany when Ostland core territory is applied.

RKM.notes: Feature-first Moskowien mask. The exposed boundary is a single historical-reference footprint, including Saint Petersburg, Astrakhan, Kalmykia, the requested middle-Volga / Mari El / Komi districts, and the Belarus districts reassigned from Ostland. Greater Finland and the Russian Arctic islands are auto-transferred when Moskowien core territory is applied.

RKK.notes: Transcaucasia plus the North Caucasus groups represented by GER_is_RKK_state, including Dagestan but excluding Astrakhan and Kalmykia/Elista.

RKNO.notes: Manual overlay releasable for occupied Norway one-click fill.

NCP.notes: North China client administration enlarged to include the northern Henan prefectural belt while ceding the Japanese direct-control ports and the Kalgan/Zhangjiakou arc to Mengjiang.

RGC.notes: The Reformed Government shifts inland to the Sichuan-Chongqing basin and absorbs the old western SIC corridor.

GNG.notes: Separate Guangdong client state retaining the Pearl River core while absorbing Hong Kong and Macao.

XIN.notes: Fragmented Xinjiang client regime replacing the old SIK owner identity.

LAO.notes: Laos split out of the old Indochina block as a Japanese client state.

CAM.notes: Cambodia split out of the old Indochina block as a Japanese client state.

CRO.notes: Italy-aligned South Slav client state baseline for tno_1962.

SER.notes: Italy-aligned Serbian client state baseline for tno_1962.

ATL.notes: Synthetic owner used for Atlantropa land and sea staging in the 1962 scenario.

SCO.notes: TNO 1962 baseline independent Scotland after the partition of Britain.

WLS.notes: TNO 1962 baseline independent Wales after the partition of Britain.

ARM.notes: TNO 1962 baseline independent Armenia released from Reichskommissariat Kaukasien.

BRI.notes: TNO 1962 baseline independent Brittany released from France.

GUI.notes: New Guizhou military clique split out of the former Yunnan sphere.

TIB.notes: Restored Tibetan frontier client state holding the Tibetan plateau, the Yushu basin, and the Geermu delegated corridor.

FRI.notes: Azad Hind in the Japanese Co-Prosperity Sphere, spanning eastern India, Bangladesh, and the northeastern frontier while remaining grouped under Southern Asia.

BRG.notes: TNO 1962 baseline Burgundy replacing Reichskommissariat Belgien-Nordfrankreich.
BRG.selected_boundary_variant_description: Current TNO-style Burgundy footprint based on Belgium, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, and Somme.

boundary_variants.label: Current TNO Initial
boundary_variants.description: Current TNO-style Burgundy footprint based on Belgium, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, and Somme.

boundary_variants.label: Franco-Burgundian War
boundary_variants.description: Expanded Burgundy after victory over France, combining the northern TNO footprint with the eastern Burgundian block.

boundary_variants.label: Early Maximum
boundary_variants.description: Maximum early-project Burgundy, extending the expanded wartime footprint deeper into eastern and northeastern France.
